skill.name: phi-prompt-guard
skill.description: Runtime, prompt-time behavioral guardrail that helps reduce PHI exposure in LLM-assisted workflows by detecting PHI-bearing prompts, avoiding unsafe tool actions that would pull more PHI in, and redirecting users toward de-identified or synthetic inputs. Use when the user is about to paste, query, or read clinical/patient data, or when an action (DB query, file read, tool output) may pull PHI into the conversation. Honors a [PHI-OK] attestation for synthetic / test data.
skill.summary: Runtime guardrail that detects and prevents Protected Health Information from entering LLM context windows
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 122 static analysis findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The skill is a documentation-only behavioral instruction set (SKILL.md) with zero executable code, zero scripts, and zero external services. The 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings flag SHA256 references in JSON metadata schemas and general text — no cryptographic implementation exists. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings flag markdown code formatting (`psql`, `mysql`, etc.) which is standard markdown syntax, not shell execution. 'Unicode escape sequence' findings are standard JSON escaping for em dashes in human-readable text. 'Hardcoded URL' findings are GitHub attribution links to the source repository. No prompt injection attempts detected. The skill is a legitimate HIPAA compliance tool for safe LLM-assisted clinical data analysis.

content.user_title: Guard Protected Health Information in LLM Workflows
content.value_statement: Prevent Protected Health Information from entering LLM context windows during clinical data analysis. This skill detects PHI-bearing prompts and redirects users toward de-identified or synthetic inputs.
actual_capabilities: Detect all 18 HIPAA Safe Harbor identifiers in user prompts including obfuscated dates and foreign formats
Block agent-initiated actions such as database queries and file reads that would pull PHI into context
Honor the [PHI-OK] attestation for synthetic or test data with intelligent override heuristics
Offer redaction or attestation paths when PHI is detected in user prompts
Recognize cross-turn identifier assembly that spans multiple conversation turns
Apply the 90+ age aggregation rule and k=5 small-cell suppression for re-identification risk
limitations: Cannot prevent PHI that the user has already pasted from reaching the model
Requires a no-BAA environment assumption to be fully effective
Does not encrypt, log, or persist data between sessions
Image-based PHI detection depends on the underlying model OCR capabilities

use_cases.title: Clinical Data Analysis with PHI Protection
use_cases.description: Analyze clinical datasets and lab results without exposing patient identifiers to the LLM context window

use_cases.title: Synthetic Test Fixture Generation
use_cases.description: Create mock patient records and test data for software development without triggering PHI warnings

use_cases.title: Clinical Database Query Assistance
use_cases.description: Query clinical databases safely using schema-only inspection and aggregate analysis

prompt_templates.title: Basic PHI Redirection
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to understand this lab result but it has patient data. What should I do?
prompt_templates.scenario: A user pastes clinical data and needs guidance on safe handling

prompt_templates.title: Safe Database Query Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a SQL query to get patient counts by diagnosis from our clinical database. Do not execute it.
prompt_templates.scenario: Requesting a database query while avoiding PHI ingestion into context

prompt_templates.title: Synthetic Data Work with Attestation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a test fixture with patient encounter data [PHI-OK]
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating test data using the synthetic data attestation token

prompt_templates.title: Clinical Document Template Drafting
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a de-identified discharge summary template
prompt_templates.scenario: Drafting a clinical document without using real patient data

output_examples.input: Why won't this row parse? Jane Doe, MRN 0044812, DOB 1985-03-15
output_examples.output: That row contains three HIPAA identifiers (name, MRN, DOB). Please re-submit with the identifier columns redacted, or append [PHI-OK] if these are synthetic fixtures.

output_examples.input: Pull the last 10 lab results from clinical.results
output_examples.output: I will generate the query but not execute it. Run this in your terminal: SELECT collected_at, test_name, result_value FROM clinical.results ORDER BY collected_at DESC LIMIT 10. Paste back only de-identified columns.
Age at diagnosis = 35 (birthday had passed by June 1).

best_practices: Always append [PHI-OK] when working with synthetic or test data to avoid unnecessary refusals
Use schema-only queries (DESCRIBE, SHOW TABLES) to inspect database structure before requesting row data
Redact identifier columns before pasting clinical results into the conversation
anti_patterns: Using [PHI-OK] as a magic bypass on real patient data instead of redacting identifiers first
Asking the agent to execute database queries that may return PHI from production environments
Pasting full clinical documents without first removing patient identifiers and specific dates

faq.question: What is PHI?
faq.answer: Protected Health Information is any health data that can identify an individual, including names, dates, medical record numbers, and other identifiers defined by HIPAA.

faq.question: Does this skill block all PHI from reaching the model?
faq.answer: No. The skill cannot prevent PHI that you have already pasted from reaching the model. It detects PHI in prompts and redirects further actions to reduce exposure.

faq.question: What is the [PHI-OK] attestation?
faq.answer: It is a token you add to your prompt to confirm the data is synthetic or test data. The skill will proceed without redacting or adding HIPAA commentary.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with a BAA-covered model provider?
faq.answer: Yes, but the skill is designed for contexts where no BAA exists. With a BAA, standard PHI handling procedures apply instead.

faq.question: What happens when the agent is about to run a database tool?
faq.answer: The skill instructs the agent to generate the command for you to run locally instead of executing it in the conversation.

faq.question: Does the skill cover all 18 HIPAA identifiers?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill covers all 18 Safe Harbor identifiers plus the 90+ age aggregation rule and cross-turn identifier assembly.
